items.front: When must local control provide taxi instructions after landing?
items.back: When compliance is required before changing to ground control, or when the aircraft must enter an active runway to taxi clear of the landing runway.

items.front: When should runway-exiting or taxi instructions not normally be issued to a landing aircraft?
items.back: Prior to, or immediately after, touchdown.

items.front: Without specific ATC instructions, what is a landing aircraft expected to do?
items.back: Taxi clear of the landing runway, but not onto an intersecting runway unless authorized by ATC.

items.front: What must the controller do if runway hold short instructions are not read back?
What may trigger a read back hold instructions request even away from a runway hold point?
items.back: The controller may initiate it for any point on a movement area when the readback is believed necessary.

items.front: What must local and ground control protect if an aircraft must enter it to clear the landing runway?
items.back: The taxiway, runway, or ramp intersection involved.

items.front: What phraseology can be used to issue a runway-exit instruction with a hold-short requirement?
items.back: Turn left at Charlie, hold short of Runway Two Seven Right.

items.front: What is the minimum altitude restriction that may be authorized for a low approach under this paragraph?
items.back: No less than 500 feet above the airport.

items.front: Over which aircraft may a restricted altitude low approach be authorized?
items.back: Over preceding landing or taxiing aircraft, but not over an aircraft holding in position or a departing aircraft.

items.front: When may a restricted altitude low approach be cleared over personnel?
items.back: Only when airport authorities have advised the personnel that the approaches will be conducted.

items.front: What must be told to the approaching aircraft when authorizing a restricted low approach?
items.back: The location of applicable ground traffic, personnel, or equipment.

items.front: What does the note say about using higher altitudes than 500 feet?
items.back: The 500-foot restriction is a minimum, and higher altitudes should be used when warranted.

items.front: What altitude example is given for super or heavy aircraft over unprotected personnel or small aircraft near the runway?
What phraseology is used for an altitude-restricted low approach?
items.back: Cleared low approach at or above (altitude). Traffic (description and location).
